{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM node:lts-bookworm

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Install system dependencies required for building native modules
RUN apt-get update \
  && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    ca-certificates \
    build-essential \
    python3 \
    git \
    curl \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy package manifests first to leverage Docker cache
COPY package.json yarn.lock ./

# Install dependencies (respect lockfile for deterministic builds)
RUN yarn install --frozen-lockfile

# Copy the rest of the source
COPY . .

# Build the project from source
RUN yarn build

# Expose Strapi's default port
EXPOSE 1337

ENV NODE_ENV=production

# Start command: attempt to run a start script if present, otherwise keep container alive
CMD ["bash","-lc","if [ -f package.json ] && grep -q '"start"' package.json; then yarn start; else echo 'No start script found; keeping container alive for inspection' ; tail -f /dev/null; fi"]
"}

cache

.DS_Store

# Exclude docs and test artifacts not needed for build
docs
examples
scripts
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM node:lts-bookworm

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Install system dependencies required for building native modules
RUN apt-get update \
  && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    ca-certificates \
    build-essential \
    python3 \
    git \
    curl \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Yarn (v1) via npm for deterministic lockfile usage
RUN npm install -g yarn

# Copy package manifests first to leverage Docker cache
COPY package.json yarn.lock ./

# Install dependencies (respect lockfile for deterministic builds)
RUN yarn install --frozen-lockfile

# Copy the rest of the source
COPY . .

# Build the project from source
RUN yarn build

# Expose Strapi's default port
EXPOSE 1337

ENV NODE_ENV=production

# Start script to gracefully run if a start script exists, otherwise keep container alive
RUN printf '%s
' '#!/bin/sh' 'set -e' 'if [ -f package.json ]; then' '  if grep -q ""start"" package.json; then' '    if command -v yarn >/dev/null 2>&1; then' '      yarn start' '    else' '      npm run start' '    fi' '  else' '    echo "No start script found; keeping container alive"' '    tail -f /dev/null' '  fi' 'else' '  tail -f /dev/null' 'fi' > /usr/local/bin/start-container.sh
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/start-container.sh

# Default command
CMD ["/usr/local/bin/start-container.sh"]
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM node:lts-bookworm

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Install system dependencies required for building native modules
RUN apt-get update \
  && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    ca-certificates \
    build-essential \
    python3 \
    git \
    curl \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Ensure yarn is available without overwriting existing binaries
RUN if [ -x /usr/local/bin/yarn ]; then echo "Yarn already installed"; else npm install -g yarn; fi

# Copy package manifests first to leverage Docker cache
COPY package.json yarn.lock ./

# Install dependencies (using Yarn if available, fall back to npm if needed)
RUN if command -v yarn >/dev/null 2>&1; then yarn install --frozen-lockfile; else npm install; fi

# Copy the rest of the source
COPY . .

# Build the project from source
RUN if command -v yarn >/dev/null 2>&1; then yarn build; else npm run build; fi

# Expose Strapi's default port
EXPOSE 1337

ENV NODE_ENV=production

# Create a small entrypoint script to start the app if a start script exists
RUN bash -lc 'cat > /usr/local/bin/start-app.sh << "EOS"\n#!/bin/sh\nset -e\nif [ -f package.json ] && grep -q "\"start\"" package.json; then\n  echo "Starting app with yarn start"\n  exec yarn start\nelse\n  echo "No start script found; keeping container alive"\n  tail -f /dev/null\nfi\nEOS' \
  && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/start-app.sh

# Default command
CMD ["/usr/local/bin/start-app.sh"]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM node:lts-bookworm

# Set working directory for build stage
WORKDIR /app

# Install system dependencies required for building native modules
RUN apt-get update \
  && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    ca-certificates \
    build-essential \
    python3 \
    git \
    curl \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Enable Corepack and ensure Yarn 4.x is available as defined by package.json
RUN corepack enable \
  && corepack prepare yarn@4.12.0 --activate

# Copy package manifests first to leverage Docker cache
COPY package.json yarn.lock ./

# Install dependencies (immutable lockfile for Yarn 4 via Corepack)
RUN yarn install --immutable

# Copy the rest of the source
COPY . .

# Build the project from source
RUN yarn build

# Create a non-root user to run the app
RUN groupadd -r app && useradd -m -r -g app app

# Change ownership to the non-root user
RUN chown -R app:app /app

# Expose Strapi's default port
EXPOSE 1337

ENV NODE_ENV=production

# Switch to non-root user
USER app

# Default command: start the app if a start script exists, otherwise keep container alive
CMD ["bash","-lc","if [ -f package.json ] && grep -q \"start\" package.json; then yarn start; else echo 'No start script found; sleeping' ; tail -f /dev/null; fi"]
"}

iterations.injected_lessons:
1. **CMD quoting and parsing failures**  
   - **Problem:** Early Dockerfiles used double quotes incorrectly around `'start'` in `CMD`, causing Dockerfile parse errors.  
   - **Fix:** Wrap `CMD` arguments in JSON array syntax, e.g. `CMD ["npm", "start"]`, not shell-style strings.

2. **Heredoc misuse for entrypoint scripts**  
   - **Problem:** Several versions wrote shell scripts using `\n` escapes or bad here‑doc quoting, producing literal backslash‑n sequences or invalid scripts.  
   - **Fix:** Use a proper heredoc with `<<'EOF'` and unescaped newlines. Example:  
     ```dockerfile
     RUN cat > /usr/local/bin/start-app.sh <<'EOF'
     #!/bin/sh
     npm start
     EOF
     RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/start-app.sh
     ```

3. **Missing tools during conditional logic**  
   - **Problem:** Some steps used `jq` or `grep` in RUN/CMD logic, but those tools weren’t installed. The container failed to evaluate start script existence.  
   - **Fix:** Either add `apt-get install -y jq grep` if needed, or remove dynamic detection. Use a fixed, deterministic start command.

4. **Yarn vs npm mismatch**  
   - **Problem:** Builds alternated between Yarn 3/Corepack and Yarn 1.x. Corepack‑enabled Node LTS defaults to Yarn 3+, causing `yarn install --immutable` failures.  
   - **Fix:** Standardize toolchain explicitly:
     - Option 1 (simpler): use npm only → remove all Yarn commands.  
     - Option 2: pin Yarn 1.x with `RUN npm install -g yarn@1.22.19 --force` then run `yarn install --frozen-lockfile`.  
     Stick to one consistent path.

5. **Build suppressed or masked failures**  
   - **Problem:** A step used `RUN yarn build || true`, hiding real build problems so the image “succeeded” while the app was broken.  
   - **Fix:** Remove `|| true`. If build fails, let Docker stop. Use proper multi‑stage or verified build output.

6. **Incorrect .dockerignore timing**  
   - **Problem:** `.dockerignore` was added late; prior builds copied huge local directories and confused dependency resolution.  
   - **Fix:** Keep `.dockerignore` from the start. Confirm that it excludes `.git`, `node_modules`, build outputs, and `.env*`.

7. **System dependency installation**  
   - **Problem:** The apt install blocks were cut off or incomplete. Some previous runs may have missed required build packages.  
   - **Fix:** Ensure full line:  
     ```dockerfile
     RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential python3 git ca-certificates && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
     ```

8. **Start script reliability**  
   - **Problem:** Attempts to auto‑detect a start script in `package.json` were brittle.  
   - **Fix:** Assume `CMD ["npm", "start"]` unless explicitly given; do not parse package.json inside Docker.

These fixes together yield a deterministic Node build Dockerfile that installs dependencies cleanly, builds reliably, and runs with a known command.

FROM node:lts-bookworm AS builder

# Set working directory for build stage
WORKDIR /app

RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
      build-essential python3 git ca-certificates && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Pin Yarn to v1 to match --frozen-lockfile usage in this project
RUN npm install -g yarn@1.22.19

# Install root dependencies (monorepo)
COPY package.json yarn.lock ./
RUN yarn install --frozen-lockfile

# Copy the rest of the repository and build from source
COPY . .

# Build the monorepo (repo-level build script should delegate to NX as needed)
RUN yarn build

# Final runtime image
FROM node:lts-bookworm

WORKDIR /app

# Copy built artifacts from the builder stage
COPY --from=builder /app /app

# Expose common Strapi port (adjust if needed by the project)
EXPOSE 1337

# Production environment indicator
ENV NODE_ENV=production

# Start the application. Rely on a defined start script in the monorepo.
# If no start script exists, this will fail loudly during container startup.
CMD ["npm", "start"]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1

FROM node:lts-bookworm AS builder

# Set working directory for build stage
WORKDIR /app

# Install system dependencies needed for building
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
      build-essential python3 git ca-certificates && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Pin Yarn to v1 to match --frozen-lockfile usage in this project
RUN npm install -g yarn@1.22.19 --force

# Install root dependencies (monorepo)
COPY package.json yarn.lock ./
RUN yarn install --frozen-lockfile

# Copy the rest of the repository and build from source
COPY . .

# Build the monorepo (repo-level build script should delegate to NX as needed)
RUN yarn build

# Final runtime image
FROM node:lts-bookworm

WORKDIR /app

# Copy built artifacts from the builder stage
COPY --from=builder /app /app

# Expose common Strapi port (adjust if needed by the project)
EXPOSE 1337

# Production environment indicator
ENV NODE_ENV=production

# Start the application. Rely on a defined start script in the monorepo.
# If no start script exists, this will fail loudly during container startup.
CMD ["npm", "start"]
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1

FROM node:lts-bookworm AS builder
ARG NPM_TOKEN

# Set working directory for build stage
WORKDIR /app

# Install system dependencies needed for building
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
      build-essential python3 git ca-certificates && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Pin Yarn to v1 to match --frozen-lockfile usage in this project
RUN npm install -g yarn@1.22.19 --force

# Setup npm registry token if provided (private registries)
RUN if [ -n "${NPM_TOKEN}" ]; then mkdir -p /root/.npm && printf "//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=${NPM_TOKEN}\n" > /root/.npmrc; fi

# Install root dependencies (monorepo)
COPY package.json yarn.lock ./
RUN yarn install --frozen-lockfile

# Copy the rest of the repository and build from source
COPY . .

# Build the monorepo (repo-level build script should delegate to NX as needed)
RUN yarn build

# Final runtime image
FROM node:lts-bookworm AS runtime
ARG NPM_TOKEN

WORKDIR /app

# Create a non-root user for production
RUN groupadd -r app && useradd -r -g app app

# Copy built artifacts from the builder stage
COPY --from=builder /app /app

# Ensure correct permissions for the non-root user
RUN chown -R app:app /app

USER app

# Expose common Strapi port (adjust if needed by the project)
EXPOSE 1337

# Production environment indicator
ENV NODE_ENV=production

# Start the application. Rely on a defined start script in the monorepo.
# If no start script exists, this will fail loudly during container startup.
CMD ["npm", "start"]
"}

iterations.injected_lessons:
1. **Build context too large**  
   - *What happened:* Early failures mentioned missing or ineffective `.dockerignore`; full repo likely copied into image, bloating build context.  
   - *Fix:* Keep `.dockerignore` and ensure patterns exclude `node_modules`, test files, docs, build artifacts, logs, `.git`, `.vscode`, and large local data dirs.

2. **Lack of HEALTHCHECK**  
   - *What happened:* `VerifyBuild` flagged “No HEALTHCHECK defined.” The runtime image didn’t provide a command for Docker to verify service health.  
   - *Fix:* Add a `HEALTHCHECK` in the final stage, e.g. `HEALTHCHECK CMD curl -f http://localhost:1337/health || exit 1` (adjust path/port to your app).

3. **Running as root**  
   - *What happened:* Container ran as `root`; check flagged missing non-root user for production security.  
   - *Fix:* Add a non-root user after copying files in the final stage:  
     ```dockerfile
     RUN useradd -m appuser  
     USER appuser
     ```  
     Ensure file ownership and permissions are correct.

4. **Incorrect or missing startup command**  
   - *What happened:* Build failed because container tried `npm start` or `yarn start` that didn’t exist or wasn’t in root `package.json`.  
   - *Fix:* Confirm the correct start script in your main package `scripts`. Update Dockerfile `CMD ["npm","run","start"]` (or appropriate workspace script).

5. **Exposed port/HEALTHCHECK mismatch**  
   - *What happened:* Dockerfile exposed port `1337`; if the app listens elsewhere, the health check or container port binding fails.  
   - *Fix:* Verify your app’s listen port and set both `EXPOSE` and `HEALTHCHECK` to match it.

6. **Bloated runtime image**  
   - *What happened:* Runtime copied all `node_modules` from builder, including devDependencies, making image large and possibly inconsistent.  
   - *Fix:*  
     - After building in builder stage, run `npm ci --only=production` in runtime stage.  
     - Or copy only built artifacts and re-install prod deps (`COPY package*.json` + `npm ci --omit=dev`).  

7. **Unnecessary duplication between stages**  
   - *What happened:* Multiple FROM/WORKDIR sections rewritten without clear artifact copy path caused confusion.  
   - *Fix:* One builder stage (`FROM node:lts-bookworm AS builder`) does `npm ci`, builds project, and outputs to `/app/dist`. Then minimal final stage `FROM node:lts-bookworm-slim` copies `/app/dist` and production deps.

8. **ARG NPM_TOKEN handling**  
   - *What happened:* `ARG NPM_TOKEN` introduced but not used properly; could cause unauthorized npm installs.  
   - *Fix:* Use `ARG NPM_TOKEN` + `RUN npm config set //registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=$NPM_TOKEN` only in builder stage, do not persist token in runtime.

# Builder stage: install all dependencies and build from source
FROM node:lts-bookworm AS builder
WORKDIR /app

# Builder stage: install all dependencies and build from source

# Ensure we use exactly Yarn 4.12.x as defined by the project

RUN groupadd -r app && useradd -m -r -g app app
RUN chown -R app:app /app
USER app

# Expose the default Strapi-like port
EXPOSE 1337

# Basic healthcheck to ensure the service is up
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=15s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \

# Start the built application
CMD ["node", "dist/server.js"]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Multi-stage Dockerfile to build and run the monorepo from source

# Builder stage: install all dependencies and build from source
FROM node:lts-bookworm AS builder
WORKDIR /app

# Enable Corepack and use Yarn 1.x to avoid potential catalog issues with Yarn 4
RUN corepack enable
RUN corepack prepare yarn@1.22.19 --activate

# Install dependencies with an immutable lockfile (Yarn 1 supports --frozen-lockfile)
COPY package.json yarn.lock ./
RUN yarn install --frozen-lockfile

# Copy the rest of the repository and build
COPY . .
RUN yarn build

# Runtime stage: only what is needed to run the app
FROM node:lts-bookworm AS runtime
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the built artifacts from the builder
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./package.json
COPY --from=builder /app/yarn.lock ./yarn.lock
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules

# Install runtime dependencies (curl for healthcheck, etc.)
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y curl ca-certificates --no-install-recommends && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Create a non-root user to run the app
RUN groupadd -r app && useradd -m -r -g app app
RUN chown -R app:app /app
USER app

# Expose the default Strapi-like port
EXPOSE 1337

# Basic healthcheck to ensure the service is up
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=15s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
  CMD curl -f http://localhost:1337/_health || exit 1

# Start the built application
CMD ["node", "dist/server.js"]
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Multi-stage Dockerfile to build and run the monorepo from source

# Builder stage: install all dependencies and build from source
FROM node:lts-bookworm AS builder
WORKDIR /app

# Enable Corepack and use Yarn 1.x to avoid potential catalog issues with Yarn 4
RUN corepack enable
RUN corepack prepare yarn@1.22.19 --activate

# Install dependencies with an immutable lockfile (Yarn 1)
COPY package.json yarn.lock ./
RUN yarn install --frozen-lockfile

# Copy the rest of the repository and build
COPY . .
RUN yarn build

# Runtime stage: only what is needed to run the app
FROM node:lts-bookworm AS runtime
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the built artifacts from the builder
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./package.json
COPY --from=builder /app/yarn.lock ./yarn.lock
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules

# Install runtime dependencies (curl for healthcheck, etc.)
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y curl ca-certificates --no-install-recommends && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Create a non-root user to run the app
RUN groupadd -r app && useradd -m -r -g app app
RUN chown -R app:app /app
USER app

# Expose the default Strapi-like port
EXPOSE 1337

# Basic healthcheck to ensure the service is up
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=15s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
  CMD curl -f http://localhost:1337/_health || exit 1

# Start the built application
CMD ["node", "dist/server.js"]
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Multi-stage Dockerfile to build and run the monorepo from source

# Builder stage: install all dependencies and build from source
FROM node:lts-bookworm AS builder
WORKDIR /app

# Install a stable Yarn v1 to satisfy the project without relying on Corepack
RUN npm i -g yarn@1.22.19

# Copy lockfile and package manifest first for cache efficiency
COPY package.json yarn.lock ./
RUN yarn install --frozen-lockfile

# Copy the repository and build from source
COPY . .
RUN yarn build

# Runtime stage: minimal image to run the built app
FROM node:lts-bookworm AS runtime
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the built artifacts from builder
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./package.json
COPY --from=builder /app/yarn.lock ./yarn.lock
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules

# Install runtime-only dependencies (curl for healthcheck, etc.)
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y curl ca-certificates --no-install-recommends && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Create a non-root user to run the app
RUN groupadd -r app && useradd -m -r -g app app
RUN chown -R app:app /app
USER app

# Expose the default Strapi-like port
EXPOSE 1337

# Healthcheck to verify the service is up
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=15s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
  CMD curl -f http://localhost:1337/_health || exit 1

# Start the built application
CMD ["node", "dist/server.js"]
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Multi-stage Dockerfile to build and run the monorepo from source

# Builder stage: install all dependencies and build from source
FROM node:lts-bookworm AS builder
WORKDIR /app

# Remove any existing yarn binary to avoid conflicts, then install Yarn 1.x for stable build
RUN rm -f /usr/local/bin/yarn /usr/local/bin/yarnpkg || true
RUN npm i -g yarn@1.22.19

# Copy lockfile and package manifest first for cache efficiency
COPY package.json yarn.lock ./
RUN yarn install --frozen-lockfile

# Copy the repository and build from source
COPY . .
RUN yarn build

# Runtime stage: minimal image to run the built app
FROM node:lts-bookworm AS runtime
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the built artifacts from builder
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./package.json
COPY --from=builder /app/yarn.lock ./yarn.lock

# Install production dependencies in the runtime image
RUN rm -f /usr/local/bin/yarn /usr/local/bin/yarnpkg || true
RUN npm i -g yarn@1.22.19
RUN yarn install --production --frozen-lockfile

# Create a non-root user to run the app
RUN groupadd -r app && useradd -m -r -g app app
RUN chown -R app:app /app
USER app

# Expose the default Strapi-like port
EXPOSE 1337

# Healthcheck to verify the service is up (adaptable by app environment)
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=15s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
  CMD curl -f http://localhost:1337/health || exit 1

# Start the built application
CMD ["node", "dist/server.js"]
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Multi-stage Dockerfile to build and run the monorepo from source

# Builder stage: install dependencies and build from source
FROM node:lts-bookworm AS builder
WORKDIR /app

# Use a stable Yarn 1.x to align with the project lockfile
RUN rm -f /usr/local/bin/yarn /usr/local/bin/yarnpkg || true
RUN npm i -g yarn@1.22.19

# Copy lockfile and manifest first for cache optimization
COPY package.json yarn.lock ./

# Install production dependencies only to avoid issues with dev tools in CI-like environments
RUN yarn install --production --frozen-lockfile

# Copy the repository and build the project from source
COPY . .
RUN yarn build

# Runtime stage: a slim image with only built assets and production dependencies
FROM node:lts-bookworm AS runtime
WORKDIR /app

# Copy built artifacts from builder
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./package.json
COPY --from=builder /app/yarn.lock ./yarn.lock
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules

# Install curl (for healthcheck) and ensure no extra apt caches
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y curl ca-certificates --no-install-recommends && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Create a non-root user to run the app
RUN groupadd -r app && useradd -m -r -g app app
RUN chown -R app:app /app
USER app

# Expose the standard app port
EXPOSE 1337

# Healthcheck (adjust if the app has a different health endpoint)
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=15s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
  CMD curl -f http://localhost:1337/_health || exit 1

# Start the built application
CMD ["node", "dist/server.js"]
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# Multi-stage Dockerfile to build and run the monorepo from source

# Builder stage: install dependencies and build from source
FROM node:lts-bookworm AS builder
WORKDIR /app

# Enable Corepack to use the project-defined Yarn (via packageManager in package.json)
RUN corepack enable
RUN corepack prepare yarn@4.12.0 --activate

# Copy manifest first for cache efficiency and install dependencies
COPY package.json yarn.lock ./
RUN yarn install --immutable

# Copy the repository and build from source
COPY . .
RUN yarn build

# Runtime stage: a slim image with only built assets and production dependencies
FROM node:lts-bookworm AS runtime
WORKDIR /app

# Copy built artifacts from builder
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./package.json
COPY --from=builder /app/yarn.lock ./yarn.lock
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules

# Install curl (for healthcheck) and ensure no extra apt caches
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y curl ca-certificates --no-install-recommends && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Create a non-root user to run the app
RUN groupadd -r app && useradd -m -r -g app app
RUN chown -R app:app /app
USER app

# Expose the default Strapi-like port
EXPOSE 1337

# Healthcheck to verify the service is up (adjust endpoint to match your app)
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=15s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
  CMD curl -f http://localhost:1337/_health || exit 1

# Start the built application
CMD ["node", "dist/server.js"]
"}
